prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response:
## Diagnosis You're right. The playground documentation is missing the `solid` variant, and more importantly, there's no structural enforcement that *requires* documentation when a new variant is added. This is a classic **documentation drift** anti-pattern (AP-072): implementation and documentation are coupled only by developer discipline, not by tooling. --- ## Root Cause **AP-072 – Documentation Drift via Voluntary Coupling** The variant definition lives in the component file, the playground lives in a separate route, and nothing connects them. When you add a variant, the build succeeds whether or not the playground is updated. There is no contract between the two. --- ## Fix ### Step 1 — Document the `solid` variant in the playground now ```typescript // app/(frontend)/playground/navigation/page.tsx (or wherever your playground route lives) import { Navigation } from "@/components/Navigation" const APPEARANCES = ["default", "ghost", "outline", "solid"] as const // ^^^^^^^ added export default function NavigationPlayground() { return (

Navigation · Variant Gallery

{APPEARANCES.map((appearance) => (

appearance="{appearance}"

))}
) } const DEMO_ITEMS = [ { label: "Home", href: "/" }, { label: "Work", href: "/work" }, { label: "Playground",href: "/playground" }, { label: "Contact", href: "/contact" }, ] ``` --- ### Step 2 — Create a single source of truth for variants Move the variant list *out of* the component's internal Tailwind map and *into* a shared constants file that both the component and the playground import. ```typescript // components/Navigation/Navigation.variants.ts export const NAVIGATION_APPEARANCES = [ "default", "ghost", "outline", "solid", // ← one place to add, everywhere picks it up ] as const export type NavigationAppearance = typeof NAVIGATION_APPEARANCES[number] ``` ```typescript // components/Navigation/Navigation.tsx import { NAVIGATION_APPEARANCES, type NavigationAppearance, } from "./Navigation.variants" // TypeScript now enforces the union; adding to the array without // adding a style branch produces a compile error. const appearanceStyles: Record = { default: "bg-background text-foreground", ghost: "bg-transparent text-foreground", outline: "border border-border bg-transparent", solid: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground", // ← new } ``` ```typescript // app/(frontend)/playground/navigation/page.tsx import { NAVIGATION_APPEARANCES, } from "@/components/Navigation/Navigation.variants" // The playground now derives its list from the same source. // Adding a variant to the constants file auto-populates the playground. {NAVIGATION_APPEARANCES.map((appearance) => ( ... ))} ``` --- ### Step 3 — Add a CI lint rule so this is never optional again ```typescript // scripts/lint-playground-coverage.ts // Run via: `tsx scripts/lint-playground-coverage.ts` // Add to package.json scripts and your CI pipeline. import { NAVIGATION_APPEARANCES } from "../components/Navigation/Navigation.variants" const PLAYGROUND_FILE = "app/(frontend)/playground/navigation/page.tsx" async function main() { const source = await Bun.file(PLAYGROUND_FILE).text() // or fs.readFileSync const missing = NAVIGATION_APPEARANCES.filter( (v) => !source.includes(`"${v}"`) ) if (missing.length > 0) { console.error( `❌ Playground is missing documentation for appearances: ${missing.join(", ")}\n` + ` File: ${PLAYGROUND_FILE}` ) process.exit(1) // fails the CI step } console.log("✅ All Navigation appearances are documented in the playground.") } main() ``` ```jsonc // package.json — add to scripts { "scripts": { "lint:playground": "tsx scripts/lint-playground-coverage.ts", "lint": "next lint && tsc --noEmit && pnpm lint:playground" } } ``` ```yaml # .github/workflows/ci.yml — already runs `pnpm lint`, nothing extra needed # But make it explicit for clarity: - name: Lint (includes playground coverage) run: pnpm lint ``` --- ### Step 4 — Codify the rule as a contributing convention ```markdown ## Design System · Adding a Variant When you add a variant (appearance, size, intent, etc.) to any Elan component, you **must** complete all three of the following in the same PR or commit. CI will fail if you do not. | # | What | Where | |---|------|-------| | 1 | Add the variant string to the component's `.variants.ts` constants file | `components//.variants.ts` | | 2 | Add the Tailwind style branch to the component's style record | `components//.tsx` | | 3 | The playground auto-renders it — verify it looks correct in the browser | `app/(frontend)/playground//page.tsx` | > The `pnpm lint:playground` script enforces step 3 automatically. > A PR that skips step 1 will produce a TypeScript error (incomplete `Record`). > There is no manual documentation step — the playground *is* the documentation. ``` --- ## Principle **Contracts beat conventions (EAP-027 – Enforce at the Boundary).** Documentation that is optional will be skipped. The fix above makes documentation *structurally impossible to skip*: 1. **TypeScript** enforces that every variant has a style — an incomplete `Record` is a compile error. 2. **The playground derives its list** from the same constants, so it self-updates. 3. **The CI script** catches the one remaining gap (a new variant rendered but not visually reviewed), making it a build failure rather than a post-merge discovery. Future variants cost one line in `.variants.ts` and the playground documents itself.
